algae_description: Dense carpet of floating plants (likely frogbit or duckweed) covering nearly the entire water surface — bright yellow-green, appearing healthy and prolific. A small clump of green stem or moss plant is visible on the substrate toward the left-center of the tank.
substrate_condition: Dark brown granular substrate (likely aqua soil or lava rock) visible across the tank floor, appears clean with no notable debris accumulation.
concerns: Water has a faint greenish-grey haze — could reflect green water (algae bloom) or be a camera/lighting artifact; worth monitoring
Surface plant coverage is extremely dense and may be blocking significant light from reaching submerged plants and substrate
A white mesh bag (likely containing crushed coral or similar buffering media) is sitting directly on the substrate — placement looks stable but worth noting as a new or modified element
narrative: Day 41 of the cycle and the tank is showing real signs of life — the surface is blanketed in floating plants, thriving in that window light, and a small clump of aquatic plant is establishing on the substrate. There's at least one shrimp visible tucked near the mesh bag toward the right-center. The water carries a faint greenish cast that could be the early stages of green water or just a trick of the light bouncing off all that surface growth — worth keeping an eye on over the next few days.

substrate_condition: dark substrate, largely obscured by floating plant coverage
concerns: Dense duckweed coverage — surface is nearly 80–90% covered, which may reduce gas exchange and limit light penetration to any submerged plants below
Two vertical equipment tubes (likely heater and filter intake/output) are visible mid-tank — confirm both are functioning and seated correctly
Shadow/dark zone in the upper-center of the surface mat may indicate a circulation dead spot where duckweed is being pushed aside by surface agitation — worth monitoring
narrative: The tank is photographed from above, revealing a strikingly dense carpet of bright chartreuse duckweed blanketing nearly the entire water surface — a vivid, almost electric green mosaic. The water beneath appears clear where visible through gaps in the mat, with a bubbling disturbance near the center suggesting active aeration or filter outflow.
algae_description: No visible algae. The surface growth is duckweed (Lemna sp. or similar), a floating macrophyte — not algae.
